hypothesis: E626/E627/E628's own deferred next step: a powered multi-seed replay (H19's protocol) sweeping required_slot_margin_decode_weight over {1,2,3,4} against a full held-out/rico_held suite, not just the n=4 ood suite used by E626-E628, before considering this margin for a default recipe change.

considered: the same n=4 ood suite E626-E628 used

a freshly built larger rico_held suite via slm data build-test --rico-hf-split test --rico-limit 2600 (would require live HF network access + a full new leakage-checked build, not tractable inside the 3-minute hard run cap alongside the sweep itself)
the union of every already-built, already leakage-checked immutable suite under src/slm_training/resources/data/eval/remediated (held_out=5, rico_held=3, adversarial=4, ood=4, smoke=3 = n=19)
suite_choice.chosen: union of all 5 committed remediated suites, n=19
suite_choice.why: This is real, already leakage-checked (37 candidates rejected against the train manifest per stats.json), disjoint held-out data already committed to the repo -- not a fresh single-suite subset, and not a same-day HF re-fetch that could not be verified/leakage-checked within the run cap. It is roughly 5x E626-E628's n=4 and includes rico_held (the suite E626/E627/E628's own deferred-next-step language named explicitly), but is still far below DEFAULT_MIN_SUITE_N=20 per suite (ship_gates.py) -- explicitly not powered, not a ship-gate scoreboard.
suite_choice.not_done: No fresh rico_held/HF build attempted. No retraining across multiple seeds -- E626's checkpoint was reused verbatim per the task's explicit apples-to-apples instruction, so this remains a single-checkpoint, single-seed (seed=0) result; H19's seed-variance/MDE machinery has nothing to estimate from one seed.

checkpoint_reused.note: Reused E626's own scratch checkpoint verbatim, no retrain, sha256 verified to match E626/E628's JSON before running.

code_changes: none -- pure sweep, working tree clean at HEAD 2caba9771422f32c0bb0b1623c2a8888f7cea7e9 for the entire run; no version bump required.

eval_recipe.exact_command_template: python -m scripts.evaluate_model --checkpoint outputs/runs/e626-required-slot-margin-scratch800-20260720/checkpoints/last.pt --model twotower --test-dir src/slm_training/resources/data/eval/remediated --suites held_out,rico_held,adversarial,ood,smoke --honest-slot-contract --slot-contract-constrained-decode --slot-contract-in-context --semantic-role-contract-in-context --semantic-role-decode-weight 8 --slot-coverage-close-decode-weight 2 --schema-value-decode-weight 4 --schema-opaque-close-decode-weight 4 --schema-role-slot-decode-weight 8 --semantic-plan-decode-weight 4 --semantic-plan-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-binding-decode-weight 1 --semantic-plan-root-decode-weight 8 --semantic-plan-root-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-repeated-array-close-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-repeated-slot-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-typed-array-nonempty-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-typed-array-item-margin-decode-weight 2 --grammar-ltr-max-tokens 160 --required-slot-margin-decode-weight <X> --run-id e629-margin<X> --run-root outputs/runs
eval_recipe.note: margin0 control reproduces E626/E628's own control on the ood subset exactly (meaningful_program_rate 0.5, placeholder_fidelity 0.55, placeholder_validity 0.73, structural_similarity 0.48855, component_type_recall 0.47916666666666663, reward_score 0.814, ast_node_f1 0.5436507936507936, ast_edge_f1 0.375, checkpoint_sha256 matches) -- confirms a faithful replay before widening to the other 4 suites.

rico_held.3: 1/3 records differs from margin=1 (rico_eval_test_25 -- cosmetic slot-position swap among structurally symmetric Card children; identical reward_score=0.997 and meaningful_program_v1=False in both variants; aggregate metrics unaffected)
rico_held.4: 1/3 records differs from margin=1 (same record, same cosmetic swap as margin=3)

ood.note: matches E626/E628's own n=4 ood-only margin=2 result exactly; ood_dashboard_01 flips meaningful_program_v1 False->True at margin>=1, matching E626/E628's own reported Dashboard fix.

pooled_n19_record_level_means.note: meaningful_program_v1_rate (the binary pass/fail gate) is EXACTLY 13/19=0.6842105263157895 in every arm (0,1,2,3,4) -- a real +1 (ood_dashboard_01) is exactly offset by a real -1 (rico_eval_test_25) at margin>=1, netting to zero movement on the binary gate at this suite/checkpoint, even though every continuous metric moves up.

new_failure_mode_found.mechanism: At margin0, five different components are correctly instantiated for five distinct role-appropriate placeholders. At margin>=1, the required_slot_margin floor (now correctly excluded from frame_depth==0 by E628, so this is a frame_depth>=1 argument-position competition, not the already-fixed root hijack) causes one Button to absorb 5 placeholders from what should have been distributed across 5 separate role-appropriate slots (':sliding_tabs.label', ':cardview.title', ':toolbar.text', ':cardview.body', ':cardview_1.title'), producing schema_value_role_mismatch failures on Button.size/type/variant and placeholder_semantic_role_mismatch on 6 placeholders. This exactly matches the failure mode E628's own 'Next step' #2 explicitly flagged as untested: 'a different checkpoint/seed could still surface a different failure mode once the floor is large enough to dominate a legitimate frame_depth>=1 argument-position competition' -- except here it surfaces on a different SUITE RECORD (rico_held) at the same checkpoint/seed, at margin=1 (not even a large margin), not merely at 'substantially larger' margins as E628 speculated.
new_failure_mode_found.significance: This is a new, real, previously-unobserved failure mode for this lever, found only because the suite was widened beyond E626-E628's n=4 ood-only set. It was invisible in E626/E627/E628 because rico_held was never included in their replay.

power_protocol_analysis.seed_variance_note: Single checkpoint, single seed (seed=0) per the task's explicit instruction not to retrain -- H19's cluster-aware seed-variance/MDE-simulation machinery has nothing to estimate from one seed and was NOT meaningfully exercised here. Only the single-arm Wilson/exact-binomial interval part of the protocol applies at this n.

supplementary_analysis_beyond_the_cli.why: analyze-existing operates on one iter JSON at a time and does not itself compute a two-arm delta; the underlying slm_training.evals.power_protocol library functions (bootstrap_paired_ci, cluster_bootstrap_ci, benjamini_hochberg) were invoked directly (same library, not a reimplementation) for a real matched control-vs-treatment comparison. Script: outputs/runs/e629-outcomes/paired_bootstrap_analysis.py; result: outputs/runs/e629-outcomes/paired_bootstrap_analysis.json (both untracked, outputs/ is gitignored).

meaningful_program_v1_cluster_bootstrap_.caveat: only 5 clusters (suites) -- the cluster bootstrap distribution is coarse/discrete at this cluster count and should not be over-read

decision: Exploratory, not confirmatory. On the widened n=19 union-of-all-committed-suites replay (still well below the n>=20-per-suite ship floor), required_slot_margin_decode_weight in {1,2} produces a real, BH-significant improvement in reward_score (+0.137, CI [0.058,0.253]) and placeholder_fidelity (+0.321, CI [0.188,0.461]) pooled across suites, with directionally positive but not-yet-significant structural_similarity and component_type_recall deltas. The binary meaningful_program_v1 gate is EXACTLY flat (13/19 in every arm) because a genuine OOD gain (ood_dashboard_01) is exactly offset by a newly discovered rico_held regression (rico_eval_test_25) -- a frame_depth>=1 argument-position over-stuffing failure mode not seen in E626-E628's ood-only replay. Margins 1 and 2 are byte-identical (0/19 differing predictions); margins 3 and 4 differ from 1/2 by exactly one cosmetic, metric-neutral slot-position swap on one record. There is therefore no evidence favoring any specific value above 1 within {1,2,3,4} on this checkpoint/suite -- if this lever is adopted, margin~1-2 (not 3 or 4) is the reasonable range, matching E626's own original recommendation. Given (a) the newly discovered rico_held over-stuffing failure mode, which itself needs its own root-cause/mitigation pass before wider adoption (the same treatment E627/E628 gave the frame_depth==0 hijack), and (b) n=19 still far short of a powered/ship-grade suite, do NOT change the shipped default (required_slot_margin_decode_weight stays 0.0). Recommend margin~2 as the next iteration's default-recipe CANDIDATE only, contingent on root-causing the rico_held regression first.
next_step: 1) Root-cause the newly discovered rico_held argument-position over-stuffing failure mode (frame_depth>=1) the same way E627/E628 root-caused and fixed the frame_depth==0 root hijack -- likely another per-position bias-stack magnitude interaction, this time between required_slot_margin_bias and whichever bias is supposed to prevent one component absorbing multiple unrelated slots. 2) A genuinely powered confirmatory pass needs either a freshly built larger rico_held (slm data build-test --rico-hf-split test --rico-limit 2600, live HF network + leakage check, not attempted here) or multiple retrained seeds (intentionally not done this session per the task's apples-to-apples instruction) to give H19's seed-variance/MDE machinery something real to estimate -- this session's n=19/single-seed result is not that. 3) binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict remains 0.0 across held_out/rico_held/adversarial/ood at every margin (smoke alone shows 0.333, unchanged by margin) -- the coverage-aware component/property closure work E620 originally named is still the next lever after both (1) and (2) above.
